title: GitHub Webhooks
overview: GitHub webhooks deliver events as asynchronous HTTP POST requests to a receiver URL that you configure. Each delivery carries the event name in the X-GitHub-Event header and a JSON event payload in the request body (Content-Type: application/json). When a secret is configured, GitHub signs the raw request body with HMAC and sends the hex digest in the X-Hub-Signature-256 (SHA-256) and X-Hub-Signature (SHA-1) headers. The receiver must respond with HTTP 200 within 10 seconds; GitHub does not automatically redeliver failed deliveries, but they can be redelivered manually or via the REST API.

endpoints.summary: ping webhook event. Sent when a new webhook is created, to confirm the configuration. Delivered as an HTTP POST to the configured webhook URL with X-GitHub-Event: ping.
star webhook event. Sent when a repository is starred or unstarred (action = created | deleted). Delivered as an HTTP POST with X-GitHub-Event: star.
push webhook event. Sent when one or more commits are pushed to a repository branch or tag. Delivered as an HTTP POST with X-GitHub-Event: push.

fields.name: action
fields.description: The specific activity that triggered the event. Present on events that have actions.

fields.name: repository
fields.description: The repository on GitHub where the event occurred. Present when the event occurs from activity in a repository.

fields.name: sender
fields.description: A GitHub user — the user that triggered the event.

fields.name: enterprise
fields.description: An enterprise on GitHub. Present when the webhook is configured on an enterprise account or an organization that's part of an enterprise account.

fields.name: installation
fields.description: The GitHub App installation. Present when the event is configured for and sent to a GitHub App.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: A GitHub organization. Present when the webhook is configured for an organization, or when the event occurs from activity in a repository owned by an organization.

fields.name: zen
fields.description: Random string of GitHub zen.

fields.name: hook_id
fields.description: The ID of the webhook that triggered the ping.

fields.name: hook
fields.description: The webhook that is being pinged.

fields.name: sender
fields.description: A GitHub user.

fields.name: action
fields.description: The action performed. Can be created or deleted.

fields.name: starred_at
fields.description: The time the star was created, in ISO 8601 format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ. Will be null for the deleted action.

fields.name: repository
fields.description: The repository that was starred.

fields.name: sender
fields.description: The user that starred (or unstarred) the repository.

fields.name: ref
fields.description: The full git ref that was pushed. Example: refs/heads/main or refs/tags/v3.14.1.

fields.name: before
fields.description: The SHA of the most recent commit on ref before the push.

fields.name: after
fields.description: The SHA of the most recent commit on ref after the push.

fields.name: created
fields.description: Whether this push created the ref.

fields.name: deleted
fields.description: Whether this push deleted the ref.

fields.name: forced
fields.description: Whether this push was a force push of the ref.

fields.name: base_ref
fields.description: No description provided in source (string or null).

fields.name: compare
fields.description: URL that shows the changes in this ref update, from before to after commit.

fields.name: commits
fields.description: An array of commit objects describing the pushed commits.

fields.name: head_commit
fields.description: No description provided in source (object or null).

fields.name: pusher
fields.description: Metaproperties for Git author/committer information.

fields.name: repository
fields.description: The repository that was pushed to.

fields.name: sender
fields.description: The user that pushed the commits.

operational.detail: Webhooks are delivered as HTTP POST to a receiver URL you configure. Content-Type is application/json (default). The X-GitHub-Event header names the event; X-GitHub-Delivery is a unique GUID; X-GitHub-Hook-ID identifies the webhook. User-Agent always begins with GitHub-Hookshot/.

When a secret is configured, GitHub signs the raw request body and sends the HMAC hex digest in X-Hub-Signature-256 (SHA-256, prefixed sha256=) and X-Hub-Signature (SHA-1). Receivers must recompute the HMAC with their stored secret and compare in constant time. No bearer token or API key is used to authenticate the delivery — the signature is the authentication.

The receiver must respond with HTTP 200 within 10 seconds; a non-200 response or a response slower than 10 seconds is recorded as a failed delivery. GitHub does NOT automatically redeliver failed deliveries; they can be redelivered manually from the UI or via the REST API.

missing: There is no fixed API base URL / server for webhook delivery — the receiver endpoint URL is configured by the integrator, so OpenAPI servers are intentionally empty.
The full schemas of the repository, sender (user), and hook objects are not enumerated here; they are captured as object-typed properties only.
Only three representative events (ping, star, push) are captured; GitHub defines many more event types and per-action payload variants.
The webhook secret length/format and the exact byte encoding fed to HMAC beyond 'the raw request body' are not further specified in these sources.
